rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF CYNTHIA MAUREEN BELLE
rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Report, Fees, Final Distribution
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 3/10/21 for an appearance on that date if issues are not sufficiently addressed. (No earlier date available.) Still no written consent by the other heir to the charitable gifts. The attachment to the administrator's claim attached to her Second Declaration still has not been supplemented to show who the payees were. The printout of the E-filing bills is not an adequate itemization of costs by the attorney, which should show what was filed to incur the costs. E-filing costs are only $5.00; it is unclear why there are so many "online service" charges or what a "read status" is. Courtesy copies of any papers filed to address these issues must be received by the Examiner no later than 3/1/21.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF LISBETH INGRID PETER
rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Fees, Final Distribution
rulings.Rulings: Hearing. Appearance required. As a result of the renewed stay home order due to the COVID-19 emergency, effective 12/6/20, counsel and parties must, whenever possible, appear remotely through Zoom or CourtCall. Please review the 8th amended general order on the court's website for information on appearing remotely. For Zoom appearance: meeting ID is 875 8017 7925 and password is 722809. For audio-only telephone appearance via Zoom, call 1-669-900-6833 and follow the prompts, using the meeting ID and password above. PARTIES APPEARING SHOULD CHECK IN FOR THE HEARING USING THEIR FULL NAME. The Court to determine if notice should be waived for the "Supplemental Declaration" that amended the second amended petition and if fees and costs are reasonable. The declaration required by LRSF 14.35 to be filed by either the attorney or the fiduciary (whoever retains the original document) must include the language required by LRSF 2.11 M.4 (providing that the original(s) will be retained "for at least the period after the termination, including all appeals, of the case required for conventionally filed documents.") The attorney's declaration filed on 1/12/21 is incomplete.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF AUDREY SUE WOO
rulings.Calendar Matter: PROBATE ACCOUNTING STATUS PETITION
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 3/10/21 for an appearance on that date if issues are not sufficiently addressed. (No earlier date available.) No proposed order. [LRSF 14.7 A.] The I&A has been filed. The executor should not need another nine months to file her petition to determine persons entitled to distribution. When did Frederick Woo die? If an estate has been opened for him, who is the personal representative? Probate Code Section 9202(b) also requires notice if a beneficiary was previously so confined. Courtesy copies of any papers filed to address these issues must be received by the Examiner no later than 3/1/21.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF PAULINE L. NAPIER
rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Report, Fees, Final Distribution
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 3/10/21. (No earlier date available.) "If the surviving spouse elects to probate assets that are distributable directly to him/her, pursuant to Probate Code Section 13502, the written statement must clearly state that he/she has been fully informed about the reasons for a probate (rather than, e.g., a Spousal Property Petition) and the consequences thereof, including potential delay and increased fees." [LRSF 14.36 I.5.] The administrator's mere allegation that Leroy Edward Napier "elected in writing to probate the assets distributable to him" is not adequate. Proof of payment is either a receipt or a canceled check, not a copy of the check that was purportedly mailed to the payee. Courtesy copies of any papers filed to address these issues must be received by the Examiner no later than 3/1/21.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF CHARLES L. JONES
rulings.Calendar Matter: ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Status On The Filing Of The Inventory And Appraisal
rulings.Rulings: Hearing. Appearance required. Issues may include (but not limited to): No inventory and appraisal has been filed since letters were issued. Executor filed inventory and appraisal prior to her appointment. Upon filing of inventory and appraisal, executor must file notice of change in financial condition per CRC 7.5(d)(1) for Court to determine whether fee waiver previously granted should remain in place. PARTIES APPEARING SHOULD CHECK IN FOR THE HEARING WITH THEIR FULL NAME. Counsel and parties must, whenever possible, appear remotely through Zoom or CourtCall. For a video appearance, go to zoom.us, click "join a meeting" and input meeting ID 875 8017 7925 and password 722809. For an audio-only appearance through Zoom, call 1-669-900-6833 and key in meeting ID "875 8017 7925#", then participant ID "0#", then password "722809#". (Toll rates may apply).) The Court prefers that counsel and parties appear by video and that they input their first and last name into the "Your Name" dialogue box. Counsel and parties may appear by audio-only telephone through CourtCall. Please review the Court's 8th Amended General Order which took effect on 12/07/2020 for more information.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF JOSEPH FRANCIS BARROS
rulings.Calendar Matter: PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION
rulings.Rulings: Hearing appearance required. Remote appearance by video or telephone should be made whenever possible due to the recent stay at home order issued by the mayor. Please review the 8th amended general order for more information. To appear by Zoom, go to Zoom.com and click Join Meeting, and use the ID and password below. Meeting ID: 875 8017 7925 Password: 722809 To appear by telephone through Zoom, call 1-669-900-6833 and follow the prompts, using the meeting ID and password above. Parties appearing should check in for the hearing using their full name. Issues: Petitioner has not responded to the following Examiner Notes: a. On page 1 the caption of the Petition Petitioner did not select Authorization to Administer Under the Independent Administration of Estates Act or with limited authority. b. On page 1 under #2c. Petitioner did not select full or limited authority. c. On page 1 under #2d. Petitioner did not select (1) or (2) or (3) d. On page 1 under #3a. Petitioner did state (place) where decedent died. e. On page 2 under 3.d. Petitioner alleges no assets. Please explain why a Petition for Letters of Administration has been filed if there are no assets. f. On page 2 under #3h. Petitioner did not select box 1 or 2 and 3 or 4. g. On page 3 under #5a. Petitioner did not select box 7 or 8. h. Petitioner has not filed Notice of Petition to Administer Estate (DE-121). i. Petitioner has not filed Duties and Liabilities of Personal Representative (DE-147). j. Petitioner has not filed Proof of Publication. k. A hard copy of the proposed order must be received by Examiner in room 202 at the Superior Court. (LRSF 14.93). [Proposed orders may not be e-filed.]
